codeReview.summary: The implementation is solid. 24 tests thoroughly cover VAL-CROSS-04 across 5 areas: agent registration via AgentRegistry, tool invocation telemetry in TaskResult, telemetry rendering in JSON/CSV/HTML exports, consumption by aggregation/reporting modules, and a full end-to-end pipeline. The harness fix for merging agent telemetry with harness telemetry is correct and well-documented. All 24 tests pass, all 40 existing harness tests pass with no regressions, and type checking is clean.

issues.description: Redundant hasattr checks: agent_telem is typed as TelemetrySchema (non-optional after the None guard), so hasattr(agent_telem, 'tokens') etc. is always True. The checks are not harmful but add unnecessary noise. A future cleanup could use direct attribute access.

The else branch (TIMEOUT/SKIPPED) still uses _merge_telemetry which replaces agent telemetry entirely with harness telemetry, losing agent tool calls and tokens. This is a pre-existing issue not introduced by this commit, but it means TIMEOUT/SKIPPED results from agents will have empty tool_calls and zero tokens. The new tests correctly avoid this path (test_telemetry_on_error_result uses ERROR status which goes through the test_result branch). This should be fixed in a future iteration.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new test file tests/test_cross_agent_telemetry.py is not included in the test_grist_mill command in .factory/services.yaml. Previous cross-eval test files (test_cross_eval_export.py) are also missing from this command. Running test_grist_mill will not exercise these cross-area validation tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:test_grist_mill command lists individual test files but omits test_cross_agent_telemetry.py and test_cross_eval_export.py

sharedStateObservations.observation: The harness had a bug where agent telemetry (tool calls, tokens, cost) was discarded during final result merge. This was discovered by the worker during test implementation. The fix (_merge_harness_and_agent_telemetry) now properly combines harness phase timings with agent metrics. This architectural knowledge about telemetry merging is worth documenting in .factory/library/architecture.md under the 'Telemetry everywhere' principle.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/grist_mill/harness/harness.py:464-507 — new _merge_harness_and_agent_telemetry function with docstring explaining the two-source merge strategy

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file uses a _MockEnvironment class (with underscore prefix) as a test helper. This follows the convention from other test files (e.g., test_harness.py). The helper functions (_make_task, _make_config, _make_mock_provider_with_tool_calls, _result_to_export_dict) are well-factored and reused across all 24 tests, which is good practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/test_cross_agent_telemetry.py:89-97 — _MockEnvironment class; lines 44-176 — shared helper functions

summary: VAL-CROSS-04 implementation is complete and correct. 24 tests verify the full pipeline from custom agent registration through evaluation harness to telemetry in reports. A genuine bug was discovered and fixed in the harness: agent telemetry (tool calls, tokens, cost) was being discarded when merging with harness phase timings. The fix is clean and backward-compatible. All tests pass, no regressions, type checking clean. Two non-blocking observations noted: redundant hasattr checks in the merge function and the pre-existing telemetry loss on the TIMEOUT/SKIPPED code path.
